title: Fix: TestRunPreflight/all_checks_pass Unit Test Failure
summary: Unit test cmd/golemic/main_test.go::TestRunPreflight/all_checks_pass fails with exit code 1. Root cause: the Preflight output includes an unexpected 'FEHLT: Credentials Scaffolding' line. The test expects only 'OK' lines followed by 'SUCCESS'. The test is correct; the code reports scaffolding as FEHLT when it should be transparent (OK). Fix the code logic in internal/preflight/preflight.go::checkCredentials to not report scaffolding status as user-visible, only token validation results.

stakeholder_intent.goal: Make unit test suite 100% green (1 test currently fails)
stakeholder_intent.business_value: Unblock downstream work; establish green baseline for Iteration 2+
stakeholder_intent.success_outcome: All unit tests pass: 'go test ./... PASS'

in_scope: Fix internal/preflight/preflight.go::checkCredentials logic to not report 'FEHLT: Credentials Scaffolding'
Verify test expectations in cmd/golemic/main_test.go match architecture intent (no changes needed)
out_of_scope: New features; this is a pure bug fix

business_rules.rule: Preflight exit code must be 0 if and only if all checks pass (report OK), exit non-zero if any check fails (reports FEHLT)
Scaffolding of .golemic/ files is transparent (should report OK, not FEHLT) after creation attempt
business_rules.applies_when: checkCredentials detects missing credentials.json and creates it
business_rules.outcome: Result should reflect validation of scaffolded file, not scaffolding action itself

interfaces.name: Preflight.checkCredentials()
interfaces.operation: Validate credentials.json; scaffolding (create if missing) is a side effect, not part of result semantics

outputs.condition: credentials.json exists and valid (dev_token, reviewer_token present)
credentials.json scaffolded and valid after creation

state_changes.precondition: checkCredentials executes; credentials.json missing but can be scaffolded

changes.value_source: From 'FEHLT: Credentials Scaffolding' (current wrong behavior) to 'OK: Credentials' (desired: report validation, not scaffolding action)

audit_requirements: Error messages must not leak token values

architecture_constraints: Must comply with Iteration 0 definition: idempotent scaffolding, transparent to user
Exit code contract: 0 = all checks OK (+ SUCCESS), non-zero = at least one FEHLT
Scaffolding is a precondition check, not a failure; only post-scaffolding validation should report FEHLT
allowed_changes: Modify checkCredentials to suppress or remove 'FEHLT: Credentials Scaffolding' reporting
Ensure only token validation results (OK or FEHLT) are reported to user

acceptance_scenarios.title: Preflight All Checks Pass (No Scaffolding Needed)
given: All prerequisites exist: gh, pi, git, .golemic/config.json, credentials.json
then:
stdout = 'OK: gh installiert
OK: pi installiert
OK: git
OK: .golemic/ Scaffolding
OK: config.json valide
OK: Credentials
SUCCESS
'

acceptance_scenarios.title: Unit Test Passes: TestRunPreflight/all_checks_pass
given: Test setUp creates valid .golemic/config.json and credentials.json
Mock executor returns success for all external commands
then: stdout matches expected output exactly (no extra FEHLT lines)

test_cases:
TestRunPreflight/gh_missing (must PASS, already passing)
  Any internal/preflight/preflight_test.go tests
definition_of_done: No new regressions in other packages

decision_log.topic: Test vs Code: Which Is Authority?
decision_log.decision: The test is correct. Preflight design (Iteration 0, architecture doc) requires idempotent scaffolding. Scaffolding files is not a failure (not FEHLT); only failed validations are. Bug is in code.
decision_log.rationale: Test expectations match architecture intent per evidence. Code has a logic bug: reports scaffolding action as user-visible failure, when it should be transparent.

codebase_evidence.finding: Test expects stdout to be all OK lines + SUCCESS, no FEHLT. Test is designed correctly per architecture.
Actual output unexpectedly includes 'FEHLT: Credentials Scaffolding', causing exit code 1 instead of 0. Bug is in code, not test.
Code calls scaffoldCredentialsIfMissing and reports scaffolding attempt as FEHLT; this is the bug. Must report validation result, not scaffolding action.
Architecture mandates idempotent scaffolding. Scaffolding files is transparent, not user-visible as FEHLT. Only failed preconditions should report FEHLT.
